name: cryptolib
description: AES encryption/decryption. Requires firmware with mbedtls crypto support. DO NOT use 'ucryptolib' — always import as 'cryptolib'.
differences_from_cpython: This module does NOT exist in CPython. It is MicroPython-specific, backed by mbedtls.

classes.name: aes

constructor_params.name: key
constructor_params.description: Encryption key. Must be 16 bytes (AES-128), 24 bytes (AES-192), or 32 bytes (AES-256).

constructor_params.name: IV
constructor_params.description: Initialization vector. Required for CBC and CTR modes. Must be 16 bytes. Not used for ECB.

methods.name: aes.encrypt

parameters.name: plaintext
parameters.description: Data to encrypt. Must be multiple of 16 bytes for ECB/CBC.

methods.description: Encrypt data. For ECB/CBC, length must be multiple of 16 bytes.

parameters.name: ciphertext
parameters.description: Data to decrypt. Must be multiple of 16 bytes for ECB/CBC.

classes.example:
import cryptolib

# AES-128 ECB mode
key = b'0123456789abcdef'  # 16 bytes
cipher = cryptolib.aes(key, cryptolib.MODE_ECB)
plaintext = b'Hello ESP32!    '  # pad to 16 bytes
encrypted = cipher.encrypt(plaintext)
print('Encrypted:', encrypted.hex())

decrypted = cipher.decrypt(encrypted)
print('Decrypted:', decrypted)  # b'Hello ESP32!    '

cbc_ctr_example:
import cryptolib
import os

key = b'0123456789abcdef'  # 16 bytes = AES-128
iv = os.urandom(16)         # random IV
cipher = cryptolib.aes(key, cryptolib.MODE_CBC, iv)

plaintext = b'Hello ESP32!    '  # 16 bytes
encrypted = cipher.encrypt(plaintext)

# Decrypt with same key and IV
cipher2 = cryptolib.aes(key, cryptolib.MODE_CBC, iv)
decrypted = cipher2.decrypt(encrypted)
print(decrypted)  # b'Hello ESP32!    '
pkcs7_pad_example:
# PKCS#7 padding for arbitrary-length data
def pad(data, block_size=16):
    pad_len = block_size - (len(data) % block_size)
    return data + bytes([pad_len] * pad_len)

def unpad(data):
    pad_len = data[-1]
    return data[:-pad_len]

# Usage
plaintext = pad(b'Hello')  # b'Hello\x0b\x0b...' (11 bytes padding)
encrypted = cipher.encrypt(plaintext)
decrypted = unpad(cipher.decrypt(encrypted))  # b'Hello'
esp32_notes: AES is hardware-accelerated on ESP32 (uses hardware AES engine).
MODE_ECB and MODE_CBC require plaintext length to be multiple of 16 bytes.
MODE_CTR does NOT require padding — encrypt/decrypt arbitrary length.
AES object cannot be reused after encryption/decryption in some firmware versions.
IV must be random and unique per encryption session (especially for CBC/CTR).
Use os.urandom(16) to generate cryptographically secure random IV.
common_bugs: Forgetting to pad data to 16-byte boundary for ECB/CBC — raises ValueError.
Reusing the same IV across multiple encryptions (security vulnerability).
Not preserving IV for decryption — decrypted data will be garbage.
Using ECB mode for multi-block data (use CBC or CTR instead for security).
